_README._comment: Documentation block, NOT a zone. Keys beginning with '_' are ignored by every consumer: zone-manager and rules-manager skip them on load, and the jq-based bash tooling selects only entries with a state/vlantag. Do not add network fields here.

isolation_invariant.rule: Tier-4 isolation zones (iot_cams, iot_untrust) are defined with 'access-to': [] and MUST NEVER appear in any zone's 'access-to'. Inbound access is granted exclusively per-module, by listing the source zone in the target's 'pinhole-allowed-from' and declaring the specific VM-IP-to-port rule in that module's install.sh.
isolation_invariant.mgmt_exception: mgmt (Tier-0 control plane) is the sole exception: it lists every standard service/client/IoT/DMZ zone, including the Tier-4 zones, in its 'access-to' for full operational visibility. No other zone may.
isolation_invariant.rationale: Adding an isolation zone to a non-mgmt 'access-to' nullifies the pinhole mechanism: every host in the source subnet would gain unconditional, zone-wide reach regardless of any module declaration. iot_cams must stay purpose-limited (GDPR Art. 25 - privacy by design); iot_untrust must stay quarantined to prevent lateral movement on compromise.
isolation_invariant.review_note: Three working-tree additions violating this invariant (iot_untrust added to srv_home/home access-to; srv_cust added to work access-to) were caught and reverted pre-commit. With the rule written here, a reviewer can distinguish an accidental violation from an intentional design change.

access_mechanisms.access-to: Baseline reachability set by the zone designer in this file. Grants the ENTIRE source subnet access to the ENTIRE target subnet.
access_mechanisms.pinhole-allowed-from: Lists the zones that MAY open per-module pinholes into this zone. The actual narrow rule (specific source VM IP -> specific port) is declared at install time by the module's install.sh, not here.

tier_model: Tier 0 - Control plane: mgmt. Egress: all zones. Ingress: no inbound pinholes.
Tier 1 - Service backends: srv_home, srv_work, srv_cust, srv_dev, srv_test, dmz. Egress: internet (+ declared IoT). Ingress: module pinholes via pinhole-allowed-from.
Tier 2 - Trusted clients: home, work. Egress: internet + own service zone. Ingress: direct.
Tier 3 - IoT controlled: iot_local, iot_cloud. Egress: iot_cloud=internet, iot_local=none. Ingress: zone-wide from srv_home/home via access-to.
Tier 4 - IoT isolated: iot_cams, iot_untrust. Egress: iot_cams=none, iot_untrust=internet. Ingress: pinhole-only, no exceptions (see isolation_invariant).
Tier 5 - Untrusted clients: guest. Egress: internet only. Ingress: no inbound pinholes.
_README.mdns_policy: Multicast service discovery (Sonos/SSDP, Chromecast) does not cross VLAN boundaries on its own. Where a trusted client or service zone must discover devices in iot_cloud, run udpbroadcastrelay to forward UDP port 1900 (SSDP) between the relevant interfaces. Referenced by iot_cloud._comment.

iot_classification._comment: Decision aid for placing a new IoT device into iot_local / iot_cloud / iot_cams / iot_untrust. Referenced by iot_untrust._comment.
split_policy_3_questions: 1. Does the device require internet to function? No -> iot_local (no egress). Yes -> continue.
2. Is the firmware/vendor trusted and kept updated? No -> iot_untrust (internet-only, quarantined). Yes -> continue.
3. Does it capture personal or sensitive data (e.g. cameras)? Yes -> iot_cams (no egress, pinhole-only access). No -> iot_cloud.

pr_review_checklist: No Tier-4 zone (iot_cams, iot_untrust) appears in any non-mgmt zone's access-to.
Every new non-internet access-to entry is justified with a named use case in the PR.
Isolation-zone access is declared per-module in install.sh (pinhole-allowed-from), not via access-to here.
Every zone has both _comment and description; operational instructions live in _comment only.

mgmt._comment: Always-on control plane. Full access to all zones for operational control. No inbound pinholes — mgmt initiates only.
mgmt.description: Control plane: hypervisors, backup, firewall, identity, cicd

srv._comment: Flat-topology alternative to srv_home/srv_work/srv_cust. Use only when zone segmentation is not required. Set state=Active to enable.
srv.description: Generic service zone — single-zone deployments only

srv_home._comment: Personal-tier services: Home Assistant, household apps. home reaches zone-wide via its access-to; mgmt via its access-to.
srv_home.description: Personal services: home automation, personal apps

srv_work._comment: Business-tier services: internal apps, productivity stack. work reaches zone-wide via its access-to. Public exposure via dmz pinholes declared by hosted modules; mgmt via its access-to.
srv_work.description: Business services: internal apps, productivity stack

srv_cust._comment: Customer and tenant sandboxes. No client zone has default access. Public exposure via dmz pinholes only. Tenants are mutually isolated by subnet.
srv_cust.description: Customer/tenant sandboxes: mutually isolated, internet-only egress

srv_dev._comment: Development environment. Combined dev+test for small teams (<10, no compliance requirement). For ISO 27001 A.8.31 or teams >10, use srv_test separately. No dmz pinhole — not publicly reachable.
srv_dev.description: Development services: internal workloads, not public-facing

srv_test._comment: QA and acceptance test environment. Separate from srv_dev to enforce dev/test isolation per ISO 27001 A.8.31. No client zone has default access. Set state=Active when a dedicated test phase is required.

home._comment: Trusted personal devices on the home network. Reaches srv_home and iot_local/iot_cloud zone-wide for consumer app compatibility (Hue, Shelly, Sonos). Does not reach srv_work — use the work zone for managed corporate access.
home.description: Trusted personal devices: laptops, phones, tablets

work._comment: Managed corporate devices. Reaches srv_work only. Set state=Disabled for pure-home deployments. For MSP/consultant deployments, srv_cust access is opt-in via deployment config.
work.description: Managed corporate devices: laptops, phones, tablets

iot._comment: Flat-topology alternative to iot_local/iot_cloud/iot_cams. Use only when IoT sub-zone segmentation is not required. Set state=Active to enable.
iot.description: Generic IoT zone — single-zone deployments only

iot_local._comment: Local automation devices: no internet, no LAN egress. Zigbee/Hue/KNX/Shelly. srv_home and home reach zone-wide via access-to; srv_home modules may create VM-specific pinholes via pinhole-allowed-from. mgmt via its access-to. (= The Hook Up's 'NoT'.)
iot_local.description: Local-only IoT: no internet, automation devices

iot_cloud._comment: Cloud-connected trusted devices: smart appliances, media (Sonos, Chromecast), HVAC/solar monitoring. srv_home and home reach zone-wide via access-to. Sonos/SSDP needs udpbroadcastrelay port 1900 (see _README.mdns_policy).
iot_cloud.description: Internet-dependent trusted IoT: smart appliances, media, monitoring

iot_cams._comment: Cameras and NVR. No internet, no LAN egress. NVR module in srv_home or srv_work reaches via explicit pinhole only — never zone-wide access-to (GDPR: access is purpose-limited). Usually wired PoE; no SSID.

iot_untrust._comment: Untrusted internet-required devices (cheap gadgets, unknown firmware). Internet-only; no LAN access; no inbound pinholes. Isolated from iot_cloud to prevent lateral movement on compromise. Set state=Active to enable. See _README.iot_classification.split_policy_3_questions.

guest._comment: Untrusted visitor devices. Internet-only; no service or device zone access; no inbound pinholes.

dmz._comment: Reverse proxy and internet-exposed services. Internet-only egress (ACME, upstream calls). Internet inbound via pinhole-allowed-from. Backends (srv_work, srv_cust) expose themselves inbound via their own pinhole-allowed-from declarations.

test._comment: Generic network probe for basic connectivity validation. Activate to verify routing and internet egress from an isolated subnet. For inter-zone rule validation use test_allow_a/test_allow_b and test_pinhole instead.
test.description: Generic test zone — basic connectivity probe (inactive)

test_allow_a._comment: Activated by firewall test.sh --deep. Bidirectional access with test_allow_b and dmz validates inter-zone allow rules.
test_allow_a.description: Test zone A: bidirectional allow validation (inactive)

test_allow_b._comment: Activated by firewall test.sh --deep. Bidirectional access with test_allow_a and dmz validates inter-zone allow rules.
test_allow_b.description: Test zone B: bidirectional allow validation (inactive)

test_pinhole._comment: Activated by firewall test.sh --deep. Only test_allow_a in pinhole-allowed-from, not in access-to — validates auto-pinhole path (see issue #173).
